howItWorks.intro: This investment return calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Return = Final Value - Initial Investment
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: What is CAGR?
faq.answer: Compound Annual Growth Rate shows the average annual return assuming reinvestment. A $10,000 investment growing to $25,000 in 5 years has a CAGR of 20.1%.

faq.question: Is CAGR the same as average return?
faq.answer: No. CAGR accounts for compounding and is more accurate. Simple average return can be misleading when returns vary year to year.

faq.question: What is a good CAGR?
faq.answer: S&P 500 historical CAGR is about 10%. Beating 10% consistently is excellent. Even 7-8% after inflation is strong long-term performance.

faq.question: Does this account for fees?
faq.answer: This uses your actual starting and ending values. If fees reduced your balance, they are already reflected in the calculation.

whenToUse.intro: This investment return calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable calculations without complex spreadsheets or professional consultations.

When comparing different financial products or scenarios side by side
